name: Sabra Crepes
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Sabra liqueur OR any brand kosher orange liqueur
recipeInstructions:
FILLING:
Thoroughly blend the sugar and 2 tablespoons of liqueur. Add this mixture to the well-beaten egg yolks to make a paste; beat until smooth (about the consistency of mayonnaise).
CREPES:
Combine milk and egg yolks. Mix flour with sugar, salt, and 1 tablespoon of sugar. Gradually add the liquid mixture to the dry ingredients, stirring thoroughly. Preheat the griddle to medium heat and lightly grease it with butter. Use about a quarter cup of batter for each crepe. The crepes should be thin and approximately 5 inches in diameter. When the edges of one crepe begin to brown, turn it over and brown the other side. When the crepe is done, place a spoonful of filling near the center. Fold over the filling at the sides and ends. Place on a heat-proof serving dish and bake in a 400°F oven for 5 minutes or until lightly browned. The crepes will puff slightly. Remove from the oven and sprinkle with powdered sugar. Pour 1 tablespoon of liqueur over the crepes, light the match, and allow the flame to burn down. Serve immediately.
